mnemonics.title: Scheduling Criteria
mnemonics.content: CUT WTR - CPU Utilization, Throughput, Turnaround time, Waiting time, Response time. SAY IT OUT LOUD: 'Cut Water' helps remember to maximize first two, minimize last three.

mnemonics.title: Scheduler Types
mnemonics.content: LONG TERM vs SHORT TERM vs MEDIUM TERM: 'LONG waits for JOB, SHORT runs the SHOW, MEDIUM SWAPS to keep balance'

mnemonics.title: Context Switch Steps
mnemonics.content: SAVE OLD, UPDATE QUEUE, SELECT NEW, LOAD NEW, JUMP: Think 'S-U-S-L-J' = 'Suspended Load Jump'

acronyms.usage: Simplest scheduling algorithm that processes arrive in order they enter the ready queue
Selects process with minimum burst time for next execution
Time-sharing scheduling where each process gets equal time quantum cyclically
Data structure that stores all information about a process including CPU registers, program counter, memory管理信息
The main processor that executes instructions of programs
Operations where process waits for data transfer to/from devices

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a LINE OF PEOPLE waiting for a BUS (CPU). People enter at back, get on bus from front. New arrivals join the back of the line.
Think of a RELAY RACE where one runner (process) passes the baton (CPU) to another runner. The baton transfer takes time (overhead).
Draw three boxes: JOB POOL (outside stadium), READY ZONE (sidelines), PLAYING FIELD (CPU). Players move between these areas.
Turnaround time = TIME FROM ENTERING TILL LEAVING STADIUM. Waiting time = TIME SPENT SITTING IN STANDS before the game starts.
Preemptive = POLICE can take your car keys anytime. Non-preemptive = You drive until YOU decide to stop and give keys.

analogies.analogy: Like a TEACHER in a classroom managing students (processes) who want to answer questions. The teacher decides who speaks next and for how long.
Like an ADMISSIONS OFFICE that decides which applicants (jobs) get accepted into the university (memory). Controls how many students enter.
Like a FLIGHT GROUND CREW that guides planes (processes) to the runway, handles the actual takeoff/landing procedures.
Like the TIME IT TAKES to SWITCH DRIVERS in a car race during pit stop. Every second in the pit is lost racing time.
Like a RESTAURANT serving CUSTOMERS per hour. More customers served = higher throughput = more efficient restaurant.
Like WAITING FOR A RESPONSE from a customer service chat. Fast first reply = good response time = happy customer.
Like a HOSPITAL EMERGENCY ROOM where critical patients (high priority) see doctors before those with minor ailments.
Like a person constantly passed over for promotion at work despite working hard. Eventually gives up or leaves - similar to process waiting indefinitely.
